title: Ultrasound device, method for forming the same and method for controlling the same
abstract: According to embodiments of the present invention, an ultrasound device is provided. The ultrasound device includes an ultrasonic transducer, and a resonance matching circuit electrically coupled to the ultrasonic transducer, wherein the resonance matching circuit is configured to cooperate with the ultrasonic transducer to generate, from an impedance of the ultrasonic transducer, an effective impedance at a resonance condition of the ultrasonic transducer to provide a gain to an electrical signal generated by the ultrasonic transducer and to reduce a noise figure of the ultrasound device at the resonance condition. According to further embodiments of the present invention, a method for forming an ultrasound device and a method for controlling an ultrasound device are also provided.
claims:
Claims 1. An ultrasound device comprising: an ultrasonic transducer; and a resonance matching circuit electrically coupled to the ultrasonic transducer, wherein the resonance matching circuit is configured to cooperate with the ultrasonic transducer to generate, from an impedance of the ultrasonic transducer, an effective impedance at a resonance condition of the ultrasonic transducer to provide a gain to an electrical signal generated by the ultrasonic transducer and to reduce a noise figure of the ultrasound device at the resonance condition. 2. The ultrasound device as claimed in claim 1, wherein the resonance matching circuit comprises electrical components having respective values designed based on the impedance of the ultrasonic transducer to generate the effective impedance to provide the gain at the resonance condition. 3. The ultrasound device as claimed in claim 2, wherein the electrical components comprise an inductor and a capacitor connected in series to ground. 4. The ultrasound device as claimed in claim 3, wherein a first end of the inductor is electrically coupled to the ultrasonic transducer, wherein a second end of the inductor is connected to a first end of the capacitor, and wherein a second end of the capacitor is grounded. 5. The ultrasound device as claimed in claim 3, wherein a first end of the capacitor is electrically coupled to the ultrasonic transducer, wherein a second end of the capacitor is connected to a first end of the inductor, and 

 wherein a second end of the inductor is grounded. 6. The ultrasound device as claimed in any one of claims 1 to 5, further comprising a low noise amplifier electrically coupled to the resonance matching circuit. 7. The ultrasound device as claimed in claim 6, wherein a magnitude of an input impedance of the low noise amplifier is larger than a magnitude of the effective impedance. 8. The ultrasound device as claimed in claim 6 or 7, wherein the low noise amplifier comprises a differential common source amplifier, or a common source amplifier, or a common emitter amplifier. 9. The ultrasound device as claimed in any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the ultrasonic transducer comprises a micromachined ultrasonic transducer. 10. The ultrasound device as claimed in claim 9, wherein the micromachined ultrasonic transducer comprises a piezoelectric micromachined ultrasonic transducer. 11. The ultrasound device as claimed in claim 10, wherein a magnitude of the effective impedance is larger than a magnitude of the impedance of the piezoelectric micromachined ultrasonic transducer. 12. The ultrasound device as claimed in claim 9, wherein the micromachined ultrasonic transducer comprises a capacitive micromachined ultrasonic transducers. 13. The ultrasound device as claimed in claim 12, wherein a magnitude of the effective impedance is smaller than a magnitude of the impedance of the capacitive micromachined ultrasonic transducer. 14. The ultrasound device as claimed in any one of claims 1 to 13, wherein the ultrasonic transducer is a band-pass transducer. 15. A method for forming an ultrasound device comprising an ultrasonic transducer and a resonance matching circuit electrically coupled to the ultrasonic transducer, the method comprising: determining an impedance of the ultrasonic transducer; determining a gain to be provided at a resonance condition of the ultrasonic transducer to an electrical signal generated by the ultrasonic transducer; and designing the resonance matching circuit based on the impedance and the gain for the resonance matching circuit to cooperate with the ultrasonic transducer to generate, from the impedance, an effective impedance at the resonance condition suitable to provide the gain. 16. The method as claimed in claim 15, wherein designing the resonance matching circuit comprises determining respective values of electrical components of the resonance matching circuit. 17. The method as claimed in claim 15 or 16, wherein designing the resonance matching circuit comprises using a Smith Chart. 18. The method as claimed in any one of claims 15 to 17, wherein the ultrasound device further comprises a low noise amplifier electrically coupled to the resonance matching circuit, and the method further comprises designing the low noise amplifier based on the effective impedance. 19. The method as claimed in any one of claims 15 to 18, wherein the ultrasonic transducer comprises a micromachined ultrasonic transducer. 20. The method as claimed in claim 19, wherein the micromachined ultrasonic transducer comprises a piezoelectric micromachined ultrasonic transducer or a capacitive micromachined ultrasonic transducers. 21. A method for controlling an ultrasound device comprising an ultrasonic transducer and a resonance matching circuit electrically coupled to the ultrasonic transducer, the method comprising: receiving an ultrasound at the ultrasonic transducer for the ultrasonic transducer to generate an electrical signal in response to the ultrasound received; and operating the ultrasound device at a resonance condition of the ultrasonic transducer so that the resonance matching circuit cooperates with the ultrasonic transducer to generate, from an impedance of the ultrasonic transducer, an effective impedance at the resonance condition to provide a gain to the electrical signal and to reduce a noise figure of the ultrasound device at the resonance condition.
